# tfe_scim_settings

Use this resource to enable, configure and disable SCIM Settings. It applies only to Terraform Enterprise and requires admin token configuration. See example usage for incorporating an admin token in your provider config.

SCIM requires SAML to be configured first, so the examples below depend on a `tfeSamlSettings` resource. While this resource exists, SCIM is always `enabled = true`; running `terraform destroy` disables SCIM.

## Example Usage

### Basic usage

Enable SCIM with default settings (unpaused, no site admin group linked):

```hcl
provider "tfe" {
  hostname = var.hostname
  token    = var.admin_token
}

resource "tfe_saml_settings" "this" {
  idp_cert         = "foobarCertificate"
  slo_endpoint_url = "https://example.com/slo_endpoint_url"
  sso_endpoint_url = "https://example.com/sso_endpoint_url"
  provider_type    = "okta"
}

resource "tfe_scim_settings" "this" {
  depends_on = [tfe_saml_settings.this]
}
```

You can also pause SCIM provisioning without disabling it:

```hcl
resource "tfe_scim_settings" "this" {
  paused     = true
  depends_on = [tfe_saml_settings.this]
}
```

### Linking a SCIM group to site admin (two-apply workflow)

The `siteAdminGroupScimId` argument links a SCIM group to the site admin role. The group must already exist in Terraform Enterprise, but groups are only created by your IdP after SCIM provisioning is enabled. This requires a two-apply workflow:

**Apply 1 — enable SCIM** so your IdP can begin pushing groups. Do **not** set `siteAdminGroupScimId` yet:

```typescript
import * as constructs from "constructs";
import * as cdktf from "cdktf";
/*Provider bindings are generated by running cdktf get.
See https://cdk.tf/provider-generation for more details.*/
import * as tfe from "./.gen/providers/tfe";
class MyConvertedCode extends cdktf.TerraformStack {
  constructor(scope: constructs.Construct, name: string) {
    super(scope, name);
    const tfeSamlSettingsThis = new tfe.samlSettings.SamlSettings(
      this,
      "this",
      {
        idpCert: "foobarCertificate",
        providerType: "okta",
        sloEndpointUrl: "https://example.com/slo_endpoint_url",
        ssoEndpointUrl: "https://example.com/sso_endpoint_url",
      }
    );
    const tfeScimSettingsThis = new tfe.scimSettings.ScimSettings(
      this,
      "this_1",
      {
        dependsOn: [tfeSamlSettingsThis],
      }
    );
    /*This allows the Terraform resource name to match the original name. You can remove the call if you don't need them to match.*/
    tfeScimSettingsThis.overrideLogicalId("this");
  }
}

```

Run `terraform apply`, then wait for your IdP to provision the desired group into Terraform Enterprise via SCIM. Locate the SCIM ID of that group (for example, from the IdP's SCIM logs or by querying `GET /scim/v2/Groups`).

**Apply 2 — link the site admin group** by adding `siteAdminGroupScimId` to the same resource:

```typescript
import * as constructs from "constructs";
import * as cdktf from "cdktf";
/*Provider bindings are generated by running cdktf get.
See https://cdk.tf/provider-generation for more details.*/
import * as tfe from "./.gen/providers/tfe";
class MyConvertedCode extends cdktf.TerraformStack {
  constructor(scope: constructs.Construct, name: string) {
    super(scope, name);
    /*Terraform Variables are not always the best fit for getting inputs in the context of Terraform CDK.
    You can read more about this at https://cdk.tf/variables*/
    const siteAdminGroupScimId = new cdktf.TerraformVariable(
      this,
      "site_admin_group_scim_id",
      {
        description: "SCIM ID of the group that should map to site admin.",
        type: cdktf.VariableType.STRING,
      }
    );
    const tfeSamlSettingsThis = new tfe.samlSettings.SamlSettings(
      this,
      "this",
      {
        idpCert: "foobarCertificate",
        providerType: "okta",
        sloEndpointUrl: "https://example.com/slo_endpoint_url",
        ssoEndpointUrl: "https://example.com/sso_endpoint_url",
      }
    );
    const tfeScimSettingsThis = new tfe.scimSettings.ScimSettings(
      this,
      "this_2",
      {
        dependsOn: [tfeSamlSettingsThis],
        siteAdminGroupScimId: siteAdminGroupScimId.stringValue,
      }
    );
    /*This allows the Terraform resource name to match the original name. You can remove the call if you don't need them to match.*/
    tfeScimSettingsThis.overrideLogicalId("this");
  }
}

```

Keep `siteAdminGroupScimId` in your config from this point on. Removing it (or setting it to `""`) unlinks the group on the next apply.

## Argument Reference

The following arguments are supported:

* `paused` - (Optional) Whether SCIM provisioning is paused. Defaults to `false`.
* `siteAdminGroupScimId` - (Optional) The SCIM ID of the group to map to site admin. Defaults to `""` (unlinked).

`paused` and `siteAdminGroupScimId` are the only mutable arguments. To fully disable SCIM you must run `terraform destroy` on this resource; there is no argument to disable it in-place.

## Attributes Reference

* `id` - The ID of the SCIM Settings. Always `scim`.
* `enabled` - Whether SCIM provisioning is enabled. Always `true` while this resource exists; use `terraform destroy` to disable.
* `siteAdminGroupDisplayName` - The display name of the SCIM group mapped to site admin. Empty when no group is linked.

## Import

SCIM Settings can be imported using the fixed ID `scim`.

```shell
terraform import tfe_scim_settings.this scim
```
